name: perf002_hoist_sorted_keys_curated_inputs
description: Curated input set for PERF-002 sorted(historical_data.keys()) hoisting. Drives semantic-identity tests (pre-hoist == post-hoist) across the five math_engine functions that currently re-sort the same dict: run_monte_carlo, compute_portfolio_cvar, calculate_20d_vol, calculate_14d_atr_pct, compute_regime_match_quality. NO producer-computed values are pinned; tests compare the production callable against an in-test reference computed from the same inputs.
provenance: Schema-derived. Input shape (history kind, day count, amplitudes) is authored to exercise eligible-pool boundary, monotone series, and constant series. Outputs are NOT included. Day counts are derived from MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS + (MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1) at test time so the boundary constants are never hardcoded.
pa18_note: No mc_prob, no cvar, no vol, no atr values are pinned. Tests assert hoisted_output == original_output where the reference is the existing production behaviour invoked through monkeypatching. The reference IS the spec.

scenarios.description: Long history (minimum + 50 days). Alternating signs exercise the kNN selection over a stationary regime.
Exactly minimum_raw_days. Pins that the eligible-pool sufficiency guard fires identically with or without the hoist.
minimum_raw_days + 1. Pins the off-by-one slice that a careless caching rewrite would flip.
Monotonically-increasing returns. Tests catch a hoist that accidentally returns a stale or reordered date list.
Constant returns. Pins degenerate-pool semantics survive the hoist.

derivation: raw_days = MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS + (MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1) + raw_day_offset_from_minimum, read from math_engine at test time. last_percent_change=0.0 and spy_today_return=0.0 mirror the existing perf001 fixture so kNN matches are mid-distribution.
